schools.summary: Victorinus: the four angels and the angel ascending with the seal of the living God point to the restraining of judgment until the servants of God are sealed and established against the great and intolerable persecution; John seeing four angels at the four corners of the earth holding the four winds, that no wind should blow on earth, sea, or tree — the restraining of judgment until God's servants are sealed (between the sixth and seventh seals, John seeing a pause and a sealing: 'four angels standing on the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, that the wind should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree'; the figure, that the 'four winds,' held back by the angels, are the destroying judgments of God, war, calamity, desolation, ready to be loosed upon the world, the four angels 'holding' them signifying that these judgments are under God's restraint, kept back for a time so that no harm comes to 'earth,' 'sea,' or 'tree' until a needful work is done, so God, in his providence, holds back the storm of judgment until his own people are made safe).

themes: four angels standing on the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds
that the wind should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree
the destroying judgments held back under God's restraint
the storm stayed until God's people are sealed

schools.summary: Victorinus: the angel ascending from the east with the seal of the living God is the herald of restoration, sealing and establishing the churches before the loosing of judgment; John seeing another angel ascend from the east with the seal of the living God, crying to the four angels who were to hurt the earth and sea — the angel with God's seal staying the judgment to mark out God's own (John seeing a further messenger: 'another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God'; the two marks of this angel and his errand, that he comes 'from the east,' the quarter of light and of God's blessing, bearing 'the seal of the living God,' the instrument by which God's people are to be marked as his own, and 'he cried with a loud voice to the four angels, to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea,' charging them to stay their destroying work; the gracious order of God's providence, that before the judgments are loosed the sealing angel goes forth to mark out and secure God's people, that they may be known and kept, so God ever cares for his own before he sends the storm).

themes: another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God
he cried with a loud voice to the four angels
the sealing angel staying the judgment
God marking out his own before the storm

schools.summary: Gill: in the historicist reading, the commission of the four angels to hurt was not yet to be carried out, nor taken away by Christ, but at the appointed time, at the blowing of the several trumpets, they let loose the winds and let in the Goths, Huns, and Vandals; the sealing of God's servants comes first; John hearing the angel say, 'Hurt not the earth, the sea, or the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads' — the staying of all harm until God's servants are marked on the forehead (the sealing angel's word recorded: 'Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads'; the purpose and manner of the sealing, that the judgments are held back until 'the servants of our God' are 'sealed,' marked out as his own and secured against the coming harm, and that 'in their foreheads,' an open, visible mark of ownership, as the Spirit seals believers and sets God's mark upon them; the comfort, that God will not let the storm fall until every one of his own is sealed and safe, so the believer is divinely marked and kept, distinguished and preserved amid the judgments that fall on the world).

themes: Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees
till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads
the staying of all harm until God's servants are sealed
the believer marked and kept amid judgment

schools.summary: Gill: John heard the number of them which were sealed, and therefore could be sure of the exact number, which did not depend upon his sight and telling them (in which some mistake might be made) but he heard the number expressed; John hearing that the number sealed was a hundred and forty-four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel — the definite, complete number of the sealed drawn from Israel's tribes (John, having seen the sealing, now 'heard the number of them which were sealed: and there were sealed an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel'; the significance of the number, that 144,000, twelve thousand from each of twelve tribes, is a definite, ordered, and complete number, marking that God knows and secures the exact tale of his own, not one missing; the debate on 'the tribes of the children of Israel,' some taking it of literal, ethnic Israel, a remnant of the Jews sealed and preserved, others of the spiritual Israel, the whole church of God under the figure of the twelve tribes, the symbolic number standing for the full and perfect number of the elect, either way the sealed being a complete and known company, kept by God).

debates.question: Who are the 144,000 sealed 'of all the tribes of the children of Israel'?

sides.position: Literal, ethnic Israel — a remnant of the Jews sealed and preserved
The spiritual Israel — the whole church of God under the figure of the twelve tribes, the symbolic number standing for the full number of the elect

themes: and I heard the number of them which were sealed
an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel
the definite, complete number of the sealed
God knowing the exact tale of his own, none missing

schools.summary: Gill: Judah is mentioned first, because Christ sprang from that tribe, and the pure worship of God was preserved in it, and it was preserved a distinct tribe until the coming of Shiloh; its name signifies 'praise God' and shows the sealed are gathered to praise; John recording twelve thousand sealed of Judah, of Reuben, and of Gad — the equal sealing of each tribe, Judah first for Christ's sake (John beginning the roll of the sealed tribe by tribe: 'of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand,' and so of Reuben and Gad; two things, first the equal number, 'twelve thousand' from each tribe, marking that none is favoured or neglected, each having its full and equal share among the sealed, and second the order, that 'Juda' is named first not by natural birthright, for Reuben was the firstborn, but because of Christ who sprang out of Juda and because the worship of God was preserved in it; Judah's name signifying 'praise,' a fitting head for the company of the sealed who are gathered to praise God, so the sealing is orderly, equal, and headed by the tribe of the Messiah).

themes: of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand
of the tribe of Reuben... of the tribe of Gad were sealed twelve thousand
the equal sealing of each tribe
Judah named first for Christ who sprang from it

schools.summary: Gill: Asher, which the Septuagint pronounce 'Aser' as here, signifies 'blessed,' as all the sealed ones are, blessed with all spiritual blessings, with grace here and glory hereafter; John recording twelve thousand sealed of Asher, of Naphtali, and of Manasses — the roll of the sealed continued, each tribe's name bearing a fitting sense (John continuing the equal sealing through the next tribes: 'of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand,' and so of Naphtali and Manasses; the commentators observing a fitness in the very names, 'Asher' signifying 'blessed,' fitting for the sealed who are blessed with all spiritual blessings, grace now and glory to come; the steady, equal continuance of the count, twelve thousand from each, the full number gathered from every tribe, so the sealing proceeds without favour or omission, each tribe yielding its appointed company of the redeemed, the names themselves whispering the blessedness of those whom God has sealed).

themes: of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand
of the tribe of Nepthalim... of the tribe of Manasses were sealed twelve thousand
the roll of the sealed continued, each tribe equal
the names fitting, 'Asher' meaning 'blessed'

schools.summary: Gill: the name of the tribe of Simeon signifies 'hearing,' and such a hearing as is attended with obedience, and denotes the spiritual and practical hearing of the gospel by those who hear, and understand, and believe, and practise what they hear; John recording twelve thousand sealed of Simeon, of Levi, and of Issachar — the roll continued, the names again whispering the graces of the sealed (John continuing: 'of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand,' and so of Levi and Issachar; the fitness the commentators find in the names, 'Simeon' signifying 'hearing,' the spiritual obedient hearing of the gospel that marks the truly sealed who hear, understand, believe, and obey, and even Levi, the priestly tribe, having its twelve thousand, none excluded from the sealed company; the steady equal count going on, each tribe yielding its full number, so the sealing of God's people is complete, orderly, and gathered from every quarter, the very names of the tribes adorned with the graces of those whom God seals).

themes: of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand
of the tribe of Levi... of the tribe of Issachar were sealed twelve thousand
the roll continued, each tribe yielding its full number
'Simeon' meaning hearing, the obedient hearing of the gospel

schools.summary: Gill: Zebulun signifies 'dwelling,' and was the tribe in which Christ had his dwelling and where he much conversed, and may denote his gracious inhabitation, as well as that of God the Father and of the Spirit, among the saints and sealed; John recording twelve thousand sealed of Zebulun, of Joseph, and of Benjamin, completing the number — the roll of the sealed brought to its full and complete sum (John closing the list: 'of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand,' and so of Joseph and Benjamin, completing the twelve tribes and the full 144,000; the fitness in the names, 'Zebulun' signifying 'dwelling,' the tribe where Christ much dwelt, fitting for the sealed in whom Christ and the Father and the Spirit graciously dwell; the completion of the sealing, that every tribe has now yielded its twelve thousand, the whole company of the sealed gathered and sure, not one tribe omitted, not one of the appointed number lacking, so the sealing of God's people is full, complete, and final, every one of his own marked and kept before the judgments fall).

themes: of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand
of the tribe of Joseph... of the tribe of Benjamin were sealed twelve thousand
the roll closed, completing the 144,000
the sealing full and final, every one of God's own marked
